programmes.show_description: Lee Mack hosts a special edition of the quiz in which the prize pot that is not won on one show is carried over to the next day's edition, meaning a potential jackpot of £500,000. What's more, anyone who makes it through to the final question automatically comes back to compete again the following day

programmes.show_name: Diet: Can You Eat Yourself Happy? - Tonight
programmes.show_description: For many of us, the food we eat to give ourselves a boost will often fall into the comfort food category. So what do experts say we should really be eating to improve our mood? Lara Lewington investigates.

While some embrace their new surroundings, one celebrity is far from impressed with their new living quarters. They aren't holding back, making their frustrations loud and clear to their fellow campmates. As night falls, there is a series of emotional and heartwarming revelations. But the peace is short-lived as the episode culminates in a showdown as one member from each team is selected by the other camp to face a bone-chilling trial that will test their courage to the limit. Ant and Dec host
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programmes.show_name: Deal or No Deal: Banker's Best Battles
programmes.show_description: Stephen Mulhern hosts a look back at some of the most iconic Deal or No Deal episodes. Matthew and Alex are given their opportunity to open the iconic red boxes this time, but do they stay strong and resist the Banker's tempting offers in the hope of being rewarded with the 100,000-pound jackpot?

programmes.show_name: STV Racing Live: Grand National Festival
programmes.show_description: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day one from Aintree, where the feature races are the 2.55 Aintree Bowl and the 4.05 William Hill Aintree Hurdle, and races also take place at 1.45, 2.20, 3.30, 4.05 and 4.40. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Brian Gleeson, Social Stable thoughts with Oli Bell and Sam Quek, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

The two camps battle it out in an epic trial to decide who will win dinner that evening and who will be on the rice and beans. The camps finally come back together in a moment which sees joy and confrontation in equal measures. Ant and Dec host

programmes.show_name: Why Are We So Tired? - Tonight
programmes.show_description: According to a 2022 survey, more than a third of us in the UK feel tired most or all of the time. So with stress, nutrition and sleep patterns all playing their part, what steps can we take to beat fatigue? Datshiane Navanayagam investigates
